slides.text: In the magical land of arithmetica, numbers weren’t just written on paper, they were lived through. Deep within the circle canopy, flowed a shimmering waterfall called fraction falls, where numbers gathered and combined. One evening, a young explorer named Luna, arrived at Fraction Falls with a map and a glowing wand. But to her surprise, the waterfall was unstable! The fractions flowing into it were separated, and Arithmetica needed them added together to restore balance.

Peaking over the edge of the falls, Luna read the first inscription carved into the rock protruding from within the falls. Only those who combine parts into a whole may restore the balance in the land of Arithmetica. The first challenge  involved the denominator stream. One carried ¼ of magical water, while the other carried 2/4 . Luna smiled, as she thought of how these fractions all ready were in the same family.

question.q: What is 1/4 +2/4? Hint: Add the numerators and keep the denominators the same

slides.text: The water shimmered as the fractions combined into one smooth stream.

Further down the falls, Luna came across two floating stones blocking the path.One weighed 1/3, the other weighed 1/6. A sign read: 'Make our denominators equal before we unite.' Luna knew she had to change the fractions so they had the same denominator.

question.q: Re-write 1/3 so it has a denominator of 6. Hint (multiply the numerator by the same number as the denominator)

slides.text: But the task wasn't complete just yet...

question.q: Now add 1/3 and 1/6 Hint: use your answer to the previous question

slides.text: The stones shifted and clicked together, before disappearing deep below the surface of the water.
For the final challenge, luna needed to calm the glowing lake of bubbling water, at the heart of fraction falls. And to do that, she needed to combine 2/5 of moonlight and 2/10 of stardust.

question.q: What is 2/5 +2/10? Hint:remember to make the denominators the same before adding the fractions together

slides.text: As the final problem was solved, fraction falls flowed smoothly once more, and balance was restored in the magical land of Arithmetica, all because of combining fractions correctly!
By helping luna, you unlocked the key to adding fractions! Once again, a quick reminder! Same denominators-> add the numerators,  Different denominators->make them the same first
